briefdescription.para: Initializes the engine, hardware device and background threads.

detaileddescription.para: This function allocates all internal memory pools and starts the background audio thread, I/O loading thread, and I/O streaming thread. It must be called once before any other engine methods are used.

simplesect.para: [Hardware Device] The engine will default to the OS's default audio device.

parameterdescription.para: The initialization settings for the engine.
The engine was successfully initialized and is ready for use.
Attempted to initialize an already initialized engine.
The operating system failed to initialize or start the audio device.

briefdescription.para: Halts all audio processing, terminates all background threads and frees allocated engine memory.

simplesect.para: [Blocking Operation] This method will block the calling thread until all background threads have fully joined.
[Handle Invalidation] Calling this method will invalidate all handles.

parameterdescription.para: The engine was successfully shut down.
Attempted to shut down an engine that is not initialized.
The operating system failed to release the hardware device.

briefdescription.para: The per-frame tick method for the engine.

detaileddescription.para: This method is the primary synchronization router between the API (calling) thread and the background threads. It must be called exactly once per frame inside the update loop of your main thread.

simplesect.para: [Callback Execution] This method processes all events happening within the engine. It is within this method that any provided AudioEventCallback and AssetLoadCallback functions are invoked.

briefdescription.para: Sets the global scaling factor for the Doppler effect for the entire engine.

parameterdescription.para: The global scaling factor. Clamped internally between 0.0 and 10.0.
The global scaling factor for the Doppler effect was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.

memberdef.argsstring: (SoundHandle &sound, SoundType type, const char *filepath, AssetLoadCallback callback=nullptr, uint32_t *outRequestId=nullptr)
memberdef.name: LoadSoundAsync

briefdescription.para: Asynchronously loads a sound into memory or prepares a file for streaming.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns immediately and does not block the calling thread. The provided sound is valid immediately if the call succeeds. If playback using the sound is attempted before the load is complete, the engine will safely defer the playback request internally.

simplesect.para: [Reference Counting] Sounds are reference counted. If the requested filepath with the same SoundType is already loaded, this function increments a reference count and returns the already loaded sound. Because of this, one must also unload a sound the same number of times as it has been loaded in order to properly unload it from the engine.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to be populated.
Specifies if the sound should be fully decoded into memory (Resident) or streamed directly from disk (Stream).
The string path to the audio asset.
Optional. If provided, this function will be called when the load finishes.
Optional. Populated with a unique ID for the async request. It will evaluate to INVALID_REQUEST_ID if the asset is already loaded.
The request was successfully queued for load or is already loaded.
The engine is not initialized.
The allocated capacity for resident sounds has been reached.
The allocated capacity for stream sounds has been reached.

briefdescription.para: Decrements the reference count of a sound and unloads it if the count reaches zero.

detaileddescription.para: If the reference count of a sound reaches zero, all active playbacks using the sound will be stopped with the exit condition ExplicitStop. The engine will also immediately abort all pending playback requests with the same exit condition.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the sound asset.
The sound was successfully unloaded or its reference count was decremented.
The engine is not initialized.
The sound handle is not recognized, or has already been freed.

briefdescription.para: Fetches the total duration (in seconds) of a loaded sound.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the sound asset.
The variable to be populated with the length of the sound asset.
The sound length was successfully fetched.
The engine is not initialized.
The sound handle is not recognized, or has already been freed.

briefdescription.para: Creates a new mixing bus.

simplesect.para: [Reference Counting] Buses are reference counted via their string identifiers. If CreateBus is called for an identifier that already exists, the engine will increment its reference count. If the bus already exists, any new parentIdentifier provided in subsequent calls is ignored and a warning is logged.

parameterdescription.para: The unique string identifier of the bus.
Optional. The unique string identifier of the parent bus (defaults to Master).
The bus was successfully created or its reference count was incremented.
The engine is not initialized.
The parent bus does not exist.
The allocated bus capacity has been reached.

briefdescription.para: Decrements the reference count of a bus and destroys it if the count reaches zero.

detaileddescription.para: If the bus is destroyed, any child buses or active playbacks that are routed to this bus are orphaned. An orphaned bus can be rerouted to a new parent bus, but an orphaned voice will immediately be stopped by the engine with the exit condition ExplicitStop.

simplesect.para: [Master Immunity] The Master bus is permanently allocated by the engine and cannot be destroyed.

parameterdescription.para: The unique string identifier of the bus.
The bus was successfully destroyed or its reference count was decremented.
The engine is not initialized.
The bus does not exist or has already been destroyed.
Attempted to destroy Master bus.

briefdescription.para: Modifies the routing topology by changing the output destination of an existing bus.

detaileddescription.para: Moves a bus and all of its attached children (buses and playbacks) to a new parent bus in the mixing hierarchy.

simplesect.para: [Hierarchy Protection] The mixing hierarchy must always remain a strict directed acyclic graph (DAG). If an attempt is made to route a bus into itself or into one of its own descendants, this function will return an error. Additionally, the Master bus cannot be re-routed.

parameterdescription.para: The unique string identifier of the bus.
The unique string identifier of the new parent bus (must be an existing bus).
The bus was successfully re-routed.
The engine is not initialized.
Attempted a route that would result in a cyclic bus hierarchy, or attempted to route Master.
Either the bus or the parent bus does not exist.

briefdescription.para: Sets the mixing volume of a bus in decibels (dB).

parameterdescription.para: The unique string identifier of the bus.
The new volume in decibels (dB). A value of 0.0 represents unity gain. The volume is automatically clamped to the engine's safe operating range (between -60.0dB and +12.0dB).
The mixing volume of the bus was successfully updated.
The engine is not initialized.
The bus does not exist.

briefdescription.para: Allocates a new DSP effect instance and initializes it with the provided parameters.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used to create all effects. The type of effect this function creates depends on the type of effect parameters that is passed into it.

parameterdescription.para: The parameter struct containing the effect settings.
The handle to be populated.
The initial parameters.
The effect was successfully created.
The engine is not initialized.
The allocated effect capacity has been reached.

briefdescription.para: Updates the parameters of an effect instance.

simplesect.para: [Params Typing] The params type must match the params type of the effect instance that the provided handle is referencing.

parameterdescription.para: The parameter struct containing the effect settings.
The handle to the effect instance.
The new parameters.
The effect parameters were successfully updated.
The engine is not initialized.
The effect handle is not recognized.
The effect handle points to an already destroyed effect.

briefdescription.para: Inserts an allocated effect into the processing chain of a specified bus.

detaileddescription.para: A bus can hold up to four effects at a time. Attached effects are processed sequentially from slot 0 to slot 3.

simplesect.para: [Exclusive Routing] A single effect instance can only be attached to one bus at a time. Attaching an effect that is already attached to a bus will forcefully detach that effect from its old bus. Doing so is generally not recommended if the bus is being played through as it can potentially cause a pop in the audio output due to bypassing the built-in effect fade-out.

[Hot-Swap] If the specified slot on the target bus is already occupied by another effect, the existing effect will be forcefully detached and replaced by the new effect. Doing so is generally not recommended for the same reasons mentioned above.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the effect instance.
The unique string identifier of the target bus.
The index in the effect rack of the bus (0 to 3).
The effect was successfully attached to the bus.
The engine is not initialized.
The effect handle is not recognized.
The effect handle points to an already destroyed effect.
The target bus does not exist.
The requested slot exceeds the rack index limit.

briefdescription.para: Removes an effect from the mix graph, regardless of which bus it is routed to.

simplesect.para: [Effect Lifetime] Detaching an effect does not destroy it.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the effect instance.
The effect was successfully detached.
The engine is not initialized.
The effect handle is not recognized.
The effect handle points to an already destroyed effect.

briefdescription.para: Destroys an effect.

detaileddescription.para: If the effect is attached to a bus, the engine will automatically detach it before destroying it.

simplesect.para: [Attached Effects] Destroying an effect that is attached to a bus that is being played through is generally not recommended as it can cause a pop in the audio output due to bypassing the built-in effect fade-out.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the effect instance.
The effect was successfully destroyed.
The engine is not initialized.
The effect handle is not recognized.
The effect handle points to an already destroyed effect.

briefdescription.para: Creates a playback instance with a sound and prepares it for playback.

detaileddescription.para: This function does not start playback.

simplesect.para: [Deferred Playback] This function will still succeed if the sound is still being loaded asynchronously.

[Playback Lifecycle] Playback instances are immediately invalidated upon stopping. Because of this, always check the return value of any playback modifying methods to make sure the handle is still valid.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to be populated.
The handle to the sound.
Optional. If provided, this function will be called when the playback terminates.
The playback instance was successfully created.
The engine is not initialized.
The sound handle is not recognized.
The sound asset previously failed to load.
The allocated voice capacity has been reached.
The allocated stream capacity has been reached.
The I/O streaming request queue is at capacity.

briefdescription.para: Routes a playback instance to output its audio into the specified mixing bus.

detaileddescription.para: This function can be called both before and during playback.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The unique string identifier of the target bus.
The voice was successfully routed.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.
The target bus does not exist.

briefdescription.para: Starts or resumes a playback instance.

detaileddescription.para: If the playback instance is paused, calling this function will resume it from the current position. If it is inactive, it will play from the start.

simplesect.para: [Deferred Playback] It is safe to call this function on a playback instance that is still waiting for its sound to load. The playback will start as soon as the sound has been loaded in.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The playback instance was successfully set to play.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.
The internal state of the playback instance has been corrupted.

briefdescription.para: Pauses a playback instance.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The playback instance was successfully set to pause.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Stops a playback instance.

detaileddescription.para: If a callback function was provided when creating the playback instance, it will be called with the exit condition ExplicitStop once the playback has been stopped.

simplesect.para: [Playback Lifecycle] Successfully calling this function invalidates the provided PlaybackHandle. A stopped playback cannot be restarted.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The playback instance was successfully set to stop.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Seeks a playback instance to a specified time offset.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The time offset from the start of the sound (in seconds). This value is automatically clamped between 0.0 and the length of the sound.
The seek was successfully performed.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Sets the mixing volume of a playback instance in decibels (dB).

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The new volume in decibels (dB). A value of 0.0 represents unity gain. The volume is automatically clamped to the engine's safe operating range (between -60.0dB and +12.0dB).
The mixing volume of the playback instance was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Sets the playback rate (speed) of a playback instance.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The new playback rate. This is clamped internally between 0.1 and 4.0 to prevent performance issues.
The playback rate of the playback instance was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Sets the stereo pan of a playback instance.

simplesect.para: [Spatial] This only affects playback instances that have spatialization disabled.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The normalized pan position. A value of 0.0 represents an equal distribution between L and R, -1.0 is full L and 1.0 is full R.
The pan of the playback instance was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Sets the looping state a playback instance.

detaileddescription.para: This method updates whether a playback instance will automatically restart upon finishing.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The looping state.
The playback instance looping state was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Enables or disables 3D spatialization for a playback instance.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
True to enable 3D spatialization, false for 2D/global playback.
The playback spatialization was successfully enabled or disabled.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Defines which listener position is used when calculating the distance attenuation for a playback instance.

simplesect.para: [Spatial only] This only affects playback instances that have spatialization enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The distance evaluation mode to apply.
The playback distance mode was successfully applied.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Sets the curve used to evaluate volume drop-off over distance for a playback instance.

simplesect.para: [Spatial only] This only affects playback instances that have spatialization enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The attenuation curve to apply.
The playback attenuation curve was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Updates the 3D world position of a playback instance.

simplesect.para: [Spatial only] This only affects playback instances that have spatialization enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The new 3D world coordinates.
The playback world position was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Defines the distance boundaries for the attenuation curve of a playback instance.

simplesect.para: [Spatial only] This only affects playback instances that have spatialization enabled.
[Safe values] The engine internally ensures that maxDistance is never less than minDistance, and that minDistance respects the engine's absolute floor limit to prevent division-by-zero.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The radius where the volume drop-off begins.
The radius where the volume drops to silence.
The playback min and max distance were successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Enables or disables the Doppler effect for a playback instance.

simplesect.para: [Spatial only] This only affects playback instances that have spatialization enabled.
[Requirements] In order for the doppler effect to be calculated, both listener and playback velocity must be supplied to the engine every frame.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
True to enable Doppler frequency shifts, false to disable it.
The playback Doppler effect was successfully enabled or disabled.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Sets the scaling factor for the Doppler effect for a playback instance.

simplesect.para: [Spatial only] This only affects playback instances that have spatialization and the Doppler effect enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The scaling factor. Clamped internally between 0.0 and 10.0.
The playback Doppler effect's scaling factor was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Sets the velocity of a playback instance.

detaileddescription.para: Velocity is used exclusively to calculate the Doppler effect. If the Effect is disabled, this value is ignored.

simplesect.para: [Spatial only] This only affects playback instances that have spatialization enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The velocity vector (in m/s).
The playback velocity was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Sets the listener routing mask for a playback instance.

detaileddescription.para: The routing mask determines which listeners are allowed to hear and evaluate the playback instance.

simplesect.para: [Spatial only] This only affects playback instances that have spatialization enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The handle to the playback instance.
The listener routing bitmask.
The listener routing mask for the playback instance was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The playback handle is not recognized.
The playback handle has already stopped playing.

briefdescription.para: Enables or disables a listener.

parameterdescription.para: The zero-based index of the listener.
True to enable the listener, false to disable it.
The listener was successfully enabled or disabled.
The engine is not initialized.
The listener index exceeds the allocated listener capacity.

briefdescription.para: Updates the complete 3D state of a listener.

parameterdescription.para: The zero-based index of the listener.
The new 3D attributes.
The listener 3D attributes were successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The listener index exceeds the allocated listener capacity.

briefdescription.para: Updates the world position of a listener.

parameterdescription.para: The zero-based index of the listener.
The new world position.
The listener world position was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The listener index exceeds the allocated listener capacity.

briefdescription.para: Sets the distance probe world position for a listener.

simplesect.para: [Usage] This position is only used for distance attenuation by playback instances with distance mode set to FromDistanceProbe.

parameterdescription.para: The zero-based index of the listener.
The new world position.
The listener's distance probe world position was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The listener index exceeds the allocated listener capacity.

briefdescription.para: Sets the facing direction of a listener.

parameterdescription.para: The zero-based index of the listener.
The forward-facing direction vector. Normalized internally.
The upward-facing direction vector. Normalized internally.
The listener orientation was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The listener index exceeds the allocated listener capacity.

briefdescription.para: Sets the velocity of a listener.

parameterdescription.para: The zero-based index of the listener.
The velocity vector (in m/s).
The listener velocity was successfully set.
The engine is not initialized.
The listener index exceeds the allocated listener capacity.
